commands.review_summary:
Adds `openclaw doctor --dry-run/--diff` and JSON preview reporting while converting many doctor repair areas into structured health-check detect/repair outputs.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection of the PR head shows the skills contribution still falls through the unsupported preview branch, and the new structured runner no longer registers bundled policy checks that current main registers. I did not run the PR branch because this is a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed because the PR has a protected maintainer label, missing contributor real behavior proof, broad doctor upgrade risk, and concrete coverage regressions to fix on the branch.

target.title: Doctor: expose dry-run preview reports
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds top-level `openclaw doctor --dry-run` and `openclaw doctor --diff` repair preview modes, with `--diff` implying dry-run.
- Allows `openclaw doctor --dry-run --json` / `--diff --json` to emit a structured preview report with findings, detect notes, repair notes, changes, warnings, effects, diffs, and skipped unconverted contributions.
- Keeps preview runs non-mutating by routing legacy config flow and UI freshness repairs through explicit skipped preview entries instead of write-capable repair paths.
- Routes converted `previewSupport: "structured"` contributions through the same structured health-check repair path for both human preview output and JSON preview output, so `--dry-run`/`--diff` do not keep exercising legacy contribution wrappers just because `--json` is absent.
- Separates preview contribution support into `structured`, `readonly`, and `unsupported`: converted repair surfaces use the structured path, gateway health remains an explicit read-only runtime probe, and unconverted repair surfaces are skipped with conversion targets.
- Adds the conversion guard for unsupported doctor contributions so dry-run/diff surfaces name what is not yet converted instead of silently pretending complete coverage.
- Does not add an oc-path dependency or synthesize `ocPath`/`oc://` output.

## Verification

- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `rg -n "ocPath|ocPaths|oc://|configPathToOcPath|enrichPreview|oc-path|parseOcPath|formatOcPath|@openclaw/oc-path|extensions/oc-path" src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/health-checks.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.test.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.ts src/commands/doctor.types.ts src/flows/doctor-health.ts src/flows/doctor-health.test.ts` (only pre-existing `HealthFinding.ocPath` / `HealthCheckScope.ocPaths` definitions in `src/flows/health-checks.ts`)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts --reporter=dot` (67 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health.test.ts --reporter=dot` (48 tests; re-run after the human-preview structured routing fix)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health.test.ts --reporter=dot` (49 tests; re-run after splitting preview support into `structured`/`readonly`/`unsupported`)
- `git diff --check`
- `git diff --check -- src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/cli/program/register.maintenance.test.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.ts src/commands/doctor.types.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health.ts src/flows/health-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src-doctor-dry-run-guard.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src-doctor-dry-run-guard-preview-support.tsbuildinfo`
- `codex review --uncommitted` (clean: no actionable correctness issues)
- `autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` (clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: top-level doctor preview/diff/json now reports structured repair previews and skipped unconverted repair surfaces without mutating config/state. Converted structured contributions use the same structured detect/repair preview path for human and JSON preview modes, preserving the long-term ability to remove legacy contribution wrappers. Read-only diagnostics are explicitly classified instead of being mixed into the structured repair conversion contract.

Real environment tested: WSL source checkout in `/tmp/openclaw-doctor-next`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused CLI/doctor/contribution Vitest coverage, touched-file oxlint, test-src tsgo, diff-check, oc-path grep, and Codex/autoreview commands listed above.

Evidence after fix: 67 focused tests passed before the consistency review; the follow-up 48-test preview/contribution run passed after changing human preview routing; the follow-up 49-test run passed after clarifying preview support states; oxlint, tsgo, diff-check, and review passed; grep confirmed this PR adds no oc-path dependency or synthesized oc-path output.

Observed result after fix: `--diff` routes through dry-run repair preview; JSON preview suppresses human notes and emits the structured preview report; human `--dry-run`/`--diff` now routes converted structured contributions through structured checks instead of legacy contribution `run()` wrappers; gateway health remains an explicit read-only preview probe; legacy/unconverted doctor areas are reported as skipped instead of mutated or hidden.

What was not tested: no Crabbox/live full doctor E2E; broad `pnpm check` was not used as proof in this Codex worktree.
